name: Natural Bridge Park
description: Walk beneath the longest natural bridge east of the Rockies, a 148-foot sandstone masterpiece.

name: Hamilton/Sullins House
description: The home of Captain A.J. Hamilton, who donated the 40 acres for the Town of Hamilton to be incorporated. It was renamed in his honor.

name: Historic Pikeville
description: Step back in time and explore the preserved history of Marion County's original county seat.

name: Blue Moon Drive-In Theater
description: Experience classic cinema under the stars at one of the few remaining twin-screen drive-in theaters in the state, located right in Guin.

name: Canoeing & Kayaking on the Buttahatchee Rive
description: The Buttahatchee River run is approximately 4.40 miles long with beautiful scenery and caves along the way.

name: Jerry Brown Pottery
description: Stroll through the heart of the county seat. Explore local shops, historical archives, and classic southern architecture.

name: R. W. Harris Store
description: The R. W. Harris Store in Winfield, (center white building) was established in 1895 and continues to operate today.

name: The Pastime Theater
description: The Pastime Theater in Winfield, Marion County, was built in 1937 and is owned and operated by the City of Winfield, which uses it as a venue for cultural and artistic events.

name: Chickasaw Indian Mounds
description: The cluster of three mounds known as the Hamilton Mounds site is largest indigenous mound site in Marion county. Alabama. The site includes three mounds along the Buttahtchee River: a large mound with a two-tiered summit, another small two tiered mound and yet smaller mound situated between the two.

name: Ernest Baxter Fite House / Hamilton City Hall
description: The Ernest Baxter Fite House is a two-story brick residence in the Colonial Revival style, located at the corner of Jackson Military Road and Thomas Street in Hamilton, Marion County, Alabama.[1] Built between 1927 and 1928 and designed by Montgomery architect Weatherly Carter, it served as the family home of Ernest Baxter Fite, a prominent lawyer, state legislator, and banker, until 1968.[1] The house is notable for its monumental Doric-columned portico, dentiled eaves, and interior features including oak floors, plaster walls, and multiple fireplaces, reflecting early 20th-century domestic architecture trends.

name: Tomb of Unknown Soldier of War of 1812
description: The Marion County Tomb of the Unknown Soldier is a historic burial site of War of 1812 soldiers located in Marion County, Alabama. It is situated roughly 1 mile south of the former county seat of Pikeville, directly on an original, preserved segment of the historic Jackson’s Military Road.

name: Brilliant Depot
description: The Brilliant Railroad Depot in the city park in Brilliant, was built in the town of Boston in the late nineteenth century and is the only depot still standing in Marion County. And was on the Illinois Central Railroad line.

name: Barking Cow Farms Petting Zoo
description: The Barking Cow Farm is a fully insured and licensed petting zoo. They have a pumpkin patch on October 1st annually. They have many exocitc animals to see including a kangaroo, zebra and a llama......and many many more!
